title: Gmail OAuth 2.0 Integration
description: Implement Gmail OAuth 2.0 flow with metadata-only read access. Store tokens in Supabase. Create Edge Function for email metadata fetching.
acceptance_criteria: OAuth 2.0 authorization code flow implemented (Google Cloud Console setup documented)
Refresh tokens stored securely in oauth_tokens table (encrypted)
Token auto-refresh logic with error handling
Supabase Edge Function 'gmail-fetch' returns email metadata (sender, subject, date, has_attachments)
Rate limiting on Gmail API calls (respect Google quotas)
Error handling: expired tokens, revoked access, network failures
Disconnect endpoint that cleans up tokens
completion_summary: Implemented full Gmail OAuth 2.0 integration. (1) supabase/functions/gmail-auth/index.ts: handles /authorize (builds OAuth URL with HMAC-signed state for CSRF protection, redirects to Google) and /callback (verifies state, exchanges code for tokens, encrypts with AES-256-GCM via crypto.subtle, upserts into oauth_tokens using service role). (2) supabase/functions/gmail-fetch/index.ts: POST endpoint, verifies Supabase JWT, decrypts tokens, auto-refreshes if expired (5-min buffer), calls Gmail API for inbox metadata (maxResults=20), handles revoked access by cleaning up the token row. (3) src/lib/api/gmail.ts: connectGmail() triggers OAuth flow via browser redirect, fetchGmailMessages() calls gmail-fetch, disconnectGmail() deletes token row via RLS-authenticated client. (4) src/types/gmail.ts: GmailMessage and GmailConnectionStatus types. Encryption key derived from OAUTH_ENCRYPTION_KEY via SHA-256; state CSRF token uses HMAC-SHA256. Human must deploy Edge Functions: supabase functions deploy gmail-auth --project-ref wwqvjiekakjetspucfxp && supabase functions deploy gmail-fetch --project-ref wwqvjiekakjetspucfxp.
notes: Google Cloud Console setup requires: OAuth consent screen, credentials, redirect URI. Document all setup steps in a SETUP.md for the developer to follow.
